summary: Focused fighter Guard-state matching PR split from #2024. It updated src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c with two stack-padding fixes and one missing helper call, bringing ftCo_Guard_Anim, ftCo_GuardOff_Anim, and ftCo_Guard_IASA-related inline behavior to reported 100% matches.

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains the ftCo Guard animation and IASA helper changes.
changes: Added ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) inside static inline bool inlineC0(Fighter_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObjEvent cb).

what_changed: In inlineC0, the code now retrieves Fighter* fp from gobj->user_data and then calls ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) before evaluating guard/reflecting conditions.
ftCo_Guard_Anim now includes PAD_STACK(8) after loading Fighter* fp, before incrementing fp->mv.co.guard.x0.
ftCo_GuardOff_Anim now includes PAD_STACK(8) after loading Fighter* fp, before incrementing fp->mv.co.guard.x0.

The PR body states ftCo_Guard_Anim and ftCo_GuardOff_Anim reached 100% by correcting stack frame layout, and ftCo_Guard_IASA reached 100% by adding the missing ftCo_80092BCC call in the inline helper.
smart_moves: Kept the scope narrow by splitting this Guard-state matching work from #2024, reducing review and regression surface.
Used PAD_STACK(8) as a minimal non-semantic matching tactic where the behavior was already correct but the stack frame layout was not.

Placed the missing ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) call inside the shared inline helper inlineC0, which likely fixed the generated code path used by Guard IASA-related behavior without duplicating the call at each use site.
Verified the build with --require-protos according to the PR test plan.
decomp_lessons: For matching fighter state animation functions, stack frame mismatches may require explicit PAD_STACK rather than logic rewrites.
Small inline helper bodies can be decisive for matching caller functions; missing calls inside an inline can affect the match status of higher-level state functions such as IASA handlers.
When a function is behaviorally close but not matching, compare both semantic calls and frame layout. This PR needed one missing call plus stack padding, not broad restructuring.
Focused PRs for one fighter state file can make decomp matching changes easier to validate and search later.
naming_conventions: Common fighter Guard functions use the ftCo_Guard_* prefix, for example ftCo_Guard_Anim, ftCo_GuardOff_Anim, and ftCo_GuardOn_Anim.
Unidentified or address-derived common fighter helpers retain names such as ftCo_80092BCC, ftCo_800925A4, ftCo_80091E78, and ftCo_80092C54.
Inline temporary helpers may use generated/local names such as inlineC0 when no stable domain name is known.
Fighter object parameters are named gobj with type Fighter_GObj*, and fighter data is accessed through Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add PAD_STACK(8) to force an 8-byte stack layout adjustment when matching ftCo_Guard_Anim.
Add PAD_STACK(8) to force an 8-byte stack layout adjustment when matching ftCo_GuardOff_Anim.
Insert missing side-effect call ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) before branch condition checks in inlineC0 to match expected codegen/behavior.
Avoid unnecessary rewrites: the diff achieved the reported matches with only three added lines.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body includes a test note that build passed with --require-protos; CI was listed but not checked in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCo_Guard functions that only need PAD_STACK fixes for stack frame matching.
Search for inlineC0 call sites to understand which Guard IASA or reflect paths were affected by adding ftCo_80092BCC.
Compare PR #2024 for additional Guard-state matching context that was split out into this focused PR.
Look for other fighter state files where missing helper calls inside static inline functions block 100% matches.
